question: UIKit is the only framework used for building user interfaces in iOS.
explanation: SwiftUI is another modern framework for building user interfaces in iOS, introduced in iOS 13.

question: Core Data is a framework for managing the model layer objects in iOS applications.
explanation: Core Data provides an object graph management and persistence framework for iOS and macOS applications.

question: UIViewController lifecycle methods are always called in a predictable order.
explanation: While there is a general order, not all lifecycle methods are guaranteed to be called in every scenario, and some can be skipped depending on the app's state.

question: URLSession is only used for making network requests.
explanation: URLSession can also handle background downloads, uploads, and other network-related tasks.

question: Core Animation is exclusively used for animating user interface elements.
explanation: Core Animation is a graphics rendering and animation infrastructure used for more than just UI animations, including complex rendering tasks.

question: Core Location framework is used only for GPS tracking.
explanation: Core Location provides a range of location services, including GPS, cellular, and Wi-Fi positioning.

question: UIKit views can be directly used in SwiftUI without any conversion.
explanation: UIKit views need to be wrapped in UIViewRepresentable protocol to be used in SwiftUI.

question: Core Graphics is a low-level drawing API for iOS.
explanation: Core Graphics provides low-level drawing capabilities for creating custom graphics and rendering.

question: UITableView is part of the SwiftUI framework.
explanation: UITableView is a UIKit component, not a SwiftUI component. SwiftUI uses List for similar functionality.

question: Core Data can only persist data to SQLite databases.
explanation: Core Data supports multiple store types, including SQLite, XML, and binary stores.

question: URLSession's dataTask method always runs on the main thread.
explanation: URLSession's dataTask runs on a background thread by default, requiring explicit main thread dispatching for UI updates.

question: UIViewController's viewDidLoad() is called every time the view appears on screen.
explanation: viewDidLoad() is called only once when the view controller's view is first loaded into memory. viewWillAppear() or viewDidAppear() are called each time the view appears.

question: Core Data can only use SQLite as its persistent store type.
explanation: Core Data supports multiple persistent store types including SQLite, Binary, XML, and In-Memory stores.

question: URLSession automatically caches HTTP responses by default.
explanation: URLSession uses URLCache by default to cache HTTP/HTTPS responses according to the cache policy and server headers.

question: UITableView's dequeueReusableCell method can return nil.
explanation: dequeueReusableCell(withIdentifier:for:) always returns a cell and crashes if the identifier isn't registered. The older dequeueReusableCell(withIdentifier:) can return nil.

question: Core Animation transactions are committed at the end of the current run loop cycle.
explanation: Core Animation batches changes and commits them at the end of the run loop cycle for performance optimization.

question: UIKit can be safely used from background threads.
explanation: UIKit is not thread-safe and must be used only on the main thread, with few exceptions like UIImage.

question: UserDefaults synchronizes changes to disk immediately after setting a value.
explanation: UserDefaults synchronizes periodically and at certain points, not immediately. You can force synchronization with synchronize(), though it's usually unnecessary.

question: Core Location requires both NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription and NSLocationAlwaysUsageDescription keys in Info.plist.
explanation: You only need to include the usage description keys for the location authorization levels your app actually requests.

question: UIScrollView's contentSize must be set explicitly for scrolling to work.
explanation: UIScrollView requires its contentSize to be larger than its bounds for scrolling to be enabled, unless using Auto Layout with proper constraints.

question: AVAudioSession is automatically configured for audio playback.
explanation: AVAudioSession must be explicitly configured with appropriate category and activation for audio playback or recording.

question: UICollectionViewFlowLayout can create both grid and list layouts.
explanation: UICollectionViewFlowLayout is versatile and can create various layouts including grids, lists, and custom arrangements by adjusting item sizes and spacing.

question: Core Bluetooth can scan for devices while the app is in the background indefinitely.
explanation: Background Bluetooth scanning is limited and requires specific background modes. The system may throttle or stop scanning to preserve battery.

question: UIView animations automatically enable user interaction during animation.
explanation: UIView animations disable user interaction by default. You must use the .allowUserInteraction option to enable it during animations.

question: Keychain data persists after app uninstallation.
explanation: Keychain data can persist after app deletion and be available when the app is reinstalled, though this behavior can vary with keychain access groups.

question: UIPasteboard content is shared between all apps by default.
explanation: The general pasteboard is shared between apps, but apps can create private pasteboards that aren't accessible to other apps.

question: Core Image filters are processed on the GPU by default.
explanation: Core Image preferentially uses GPU processing for better performance, though it can fall back to CPU processing when necessary.

question: UINavigationController maintains a strong reference to all view controllers in its stack.
explanation: UINavigationController keeps strong references to all view controllers in its navigation stack until they are popped.

question: NotificationCenter delivers notifications synchronously on the posting thread.
explanation: NotificationCenter delivers notifications synchronously on the same thread where post() is called, unless using notification queues.

question: UIDocument automatically handles conflict resolution for iCloud documents.
explanation: UIDocument provides built-in conflict detection and resolution mechanisms for documents stored in iCloud.

question: CADisplayLink is guaranteed to fire at exactly 60 times per second.
explanation: CADisplayLink synchronizes with the display refresh rate, which varies by device (60Hz, 120Hz ProMotion) and can skip frames under load.

question: UIApplication.shared can be accessed from app extensions.
explanation: App extensions run in separate processes and cannot access UIApplication.shared, which is only available in the main app.

question: Core Motion requires device motion updates to be started on the main thread.
explanation: Core Motion updates can be started from any thread, and you can specify which queue should handle the updates.

question: UITextView automatically detects and makes links tappable when dataDetectorTypes is set.
explanation: UITextView can automatically detect and create tappable links for URLs, phone numbers, addresses, etc. when dataDetectorTypes is configured.

question: Core Graphics coordinate system has its origin at the top-left corner like UIKit.
explanation: Core Graphics uses a coordinate system with origin at bottom-left, while UIKit uses top-left. This requires coordinate transformation when mixing the two.

question: UIStackView directly renders its arranged subviews.
explanation: UIStackView is a non-rendering view that only manages the layout of its arranged subviews using Auto Layout constraints.

question: SwiftUI views are structs.
explanation: SwiftUI views are value types (structs) rather than classes. This design choice supports efficient diffing and recomposition of the view hierarchy.

question: viewDidLoad() is called only once per UIViewController instance creation.
explanation: viewDidLoad() is called after the view has been loaded into memory for the first time and is only called once per view controller instance lifecycle.

question: UITableView has been deprecated in favor of UICollectionView.
explanation: UITableView has not been deprecated. Both UITableView and UICollectionView are still valid choices in UIKit, though UICollectionView gained list layout support in iOS 14.

question: Core Data is thread-safe by default.
explanation: Core Data is not thread-safe. NSManagedObjectContext must be used on the thread or queue it was created on, and separate contexts are needed for background operations.

question: WKWebView replaces the deprecated UIWebView for displaying web content in iOS apps.
explanation: UIWebView was deprecated in iOS 8 and removed from iOS 14. WKWebView is the recommended replacement, offering better performance and security.

question: TestFlight beta distribution requires an App Store Connect account.
explanation: TestFlight is Apple's beta testing platform and is fully integrated with App Store Connect, requiring an account to distribute builds.

question: Apple's Combine framework can be used together with UIKit.
explanation: Combine works with any Swift code, including UIKit. Developers can use Combine publishers to drive UIKit updates alongside SwiftUI.

question: UIStackView uses Auto Layout constraints internally to arrange its views.
explanation: UIStackView is a non-rendering view that creates and manages Auto Layout constraints for its arranged subviews automatically.

question: App Store review is required before external testers can access a build via TestFlight.
explanation: External TestFlight builds must pass a review by Apple before they can be distributed to external testers, though internal testers can access builds without review.

question: Swift Package Manager can be used to add third-party dependencies to an Xcode project.
explanation: Swift Package Manager (SPM) is integrated into Xcode and can be used to add, update, and remove package dependencies directly from within the IDE.

question: SceneDelegate is required in all iOS 13+ applications.
explanation: SceneDelegate is not required. Apps can opt out of the scene-based lifecycle by removing the scene manifest from Info.plist and reverting to the traditional AppDelegate-only lifecycle.

question: UI updates in iOS must be performed on the main thread.
explanation: UIKit is not thread-safe, and all UI updates must be dispatched to the main thread. Performing UI changes on background threads can lead to undefined behavior and crashes.

question: The Codable protocol in Swift works with both JSON and Property List encoders/decoders.
explanation: Codable types can be used with JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder for JSON and PropertyListEncoder/PropertyListDecoder for property lists without any changes to the model.

question: Background fetch in iOS runs on a precise schedule as configured by the app.
explanation: Background fetch timing is determined by the operating system based on usage patterns and battery life. Apps cannot set an exact interval; iOS decides when to invoke background fetch.

question: The @StateObject property wrapper in SwiftUI creates and owns the lifecycle of an ObservableObject.
explanation: @StateObject creates an ObservableObject and owns it for the lifetime of the view, while @ObservedObject receives an already-created object from outside.

question: In SwiftUI, NavigationView has been replaced by NavigationStack in iOS 16+.
explanation: NavigationStack and NavigationSplitView were introduced in iOS 16 as replacements for NavigationView, offering more control over the navigation path.

question: UIView's layoutSubviews() is guaranteed to be called only once per view's lifetime.
explanation: layoutSubviews() is called multiple times throughout a view's lifetime, including during rotation, size changes, and whenever setNeedsLayout() is called.

question: In SwiftUI, @Environment is used to read values from the environment passed down through the view hierarchy.
explanation: @Environment reads a value from the SwiftUI environment, which is a dictionary of values passed implicitly through the view tree.

question: Instruments is a performance analysis tool included with Xcode.
explanation: Instruments is Apple's profiling and performance analysis tool built into Xcode, used to detect memory leaks, CPU usage, and other performance issues.

question: A SwiftUI List view requires explicitly calling reloadData() to refresh its content.
explanation: SwiftUI's List is declarative and automatically updates its content when the underlying state or data source changes, unlike UITableView's reloadData().

question: The Combine framework's sink subscriber can receive both values and completion events.
explanation: The sink subscriber has two closures: receiveCompletion for handling the finished or failure event, and receiveValue for handling emitted values.

question: In iOS, XCTest is the framework used for writing unit and UI tests.
explanation: XCTest is Apple's testing framework integrated with Xcode, supporting unit tests (XCTestCase) and UI tests (XCUIApplication).

question: SwiftUI previews require a connected device or simulator to render.
explanation: SwiftUI previews render directly within Xcode using a canvas without needing a connected device or running simulator.

question: Grand Central Dispatch (GCD) allows executing tasks concurrently on different queues.
explanation: GCD provides a C-level API for submitting work to dispatch queues that run concurrently on system-managed thread pools.

question: In iOS, the App Transport Security (ATS) policy blocks plain HTTP connections by default.
explanation: ATS enforces HTTPS connections by default since iOS 9. Plain HTTP requests are blocked unless an exception is added to the app's Info.plist.

question: In iOS development, UIViewController is primarily used to manage a screen's lifecycle and coordinate its view hierarchy.
explanation: True. UIViewController is used to manage a screen's lifecycle and coordinate its view hierarchy.

question: In iOS development, UINavigationController is primarily used to manage a stack of view controllers for hierarchical navigation.
explanation: True. UINavigationController is used to manage a stack of view controllers for hierarchical navigation.

question: In iOS development, UITableView is primarily used to display vertically scrolling rows of reusable cells.
explanation: True. UITableView is used to display vertically scrolling rows of reusable cells.

question: In iOS development, UICollectionView is primarily used to display customizable grids or lists of reusable items.
explanation: True. UICollectionView is used to display customizable grids or lists of reusable items.

question: In iOS development, Auto Layout is primarily used to position and size UI elements using constraints.
explanation: True. Auto Layout is used to position and size UI elements using constraints.

question: In iOS development, URLSession is primarily used to perform HTTP networking and data transfer tasks.
explanation: True. URLSession is used to perform HTTP networking and data transfer tasks.

question: In iOS development, UserDefaults is primarily used to persist lightweight user settings and preferences.
explanation: True. UserDefaults is used to persist lightweight user settings and preferences.

question: In iOS development, Keychain is primarily used to store sensitive credentials securely on the device.
explanation: True. Keychain is used to store sensitive credentials securely on the device.

question: In iOS development, Core Data is primarily used to manage object graphs and local persistence.
explanation: True. Core Data is used to manage object graphs and local persistence.

question: In iOS development, NotificationCenter is primarily used to broadcast in-process events to observers.
explanation: True. NotificationCenter is used to broadcast in-process events to observers.

question: In iOS development, BackgroundTasks is primarily used to schedule eligible work for execution when the system allows it.
explanation: True. BackgroundTasks is used to schedule eligible work for execution when the system allows it.

question: In iOS development, AppDelegate is primarily used to respond to app-level lifecycle events and configuration.
explanation: True. AppDelegate is used to respond to app-level lifecycle events and configuration.

question: In iOS development, SceneDelegate is primarily used to manage the lifecycle of a UI scene in multi-window apps.
explanation: True. SceneDelegate is used to manage the lifecycle of a UI scene in multi-window apps.

question: In iOS development, UIView is primarily used to render visible UI elements and handle layout and events.
explanation: True. UIView is used to render visible UI elements and handle layout and events.

question: In iOS development, CALayer is primarily used to back a view's rendering, animations, and compositing properties.
explanation: True. CALayer is used to back a view's rendering, animations, and compositing properties.

question: In iOS development, UIApplication is primarily used to represent the running app and coordinate high-level app behavior.
explanation: True. UIApplication is used to represent the running app and coordinate high-level app behavior.

question: In iOS development, UNUserNotificationCenter is primarily used to manage local and remote notification requests and permissions.
explanation: True. UNUserNotificationCenter is used to manage local and remote notification requests and permissions.

question: In iOS development, Core Location is primarily used to access device location and heading services.
explanation: True. Core Location is used to access device location and heading services.

question: In iOS development, AVFoundation is primarily used to work with audio, video, and camera capabilities.
explanation: True. AVFoundation is used to work with audio, video, and camera capabilities.

question: In iOS development, WKWebView is primarily used to embed and control web content inside an app.
explanation: True. WKWebView is used to embed and control web content inside an app.

question: In iOS development, Bundle is primarily used to locate app resources and metadata packaged with the app.
explanation: True. Bundle is used to locate app resources and metadata packaged with the app.

question: In iOS development, Info.plist is primarily used to declare configuration values and usage descriptions for the app.
explanation: True. Info.plist is used to declare configuration values and usage descriptions for the app.

question: In iOS development, UIAccessibility is primarily used to expose UI information to assistive technologies.
explanation: True. UIAccessibility is used to expose UI information to assistive technologies.

question: In iOS development, Main thread is primarily used to run UI updates and user-interaction work safely.
explanation: True. Main thread is used to run UI updates and user-interaction work safely.

question: In iOS development, reuseIdentifier is primarily used to enable cells and reusable views to be recycled efficiently.
explanation: True. reuseIdentifier is used to enable cells and reusable views to be recycled efficiently.

question: In iOS development, UIViewController is primarily used to display customizable grids or lists of reusable items.
explanation: False. UIViewController is actually used to manage a screen's lifecycle and coordinate its view hierarchy, not to display customizable grids or lists of reusable items.

question: In iOS development, UINavigationController is primarily used to schedule eligible work for execution when the system allows it.
